# Transcrição: 09-Daily Practice- Surprise

- **Fonte:** 09-Daily Practice- Surprise
- **Duração:** 03:59
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:08]

All right, with all things, we have to practice. And frankly, surprise is one of the more challenging concepts to master. So I want you to practice often. It's a simple thing I want you to do. When you're watching a movie, when you're watching a television show, when you're reading a book, when you're watching commercials, when you're watching commercials, pay attention to those, identify the moments that you're surprised, and then pause and ask yourself, how did they surprise me? What did they do to trick me into thinking one thing when I should have been thinking the other? Every time I'm watching a movie and I am genuinely surprised, I stop and say, all right, let me put that puzzle together. Let me find the equation that they created that resulted in surprise. What information did they teach me? What information did they need to provide to me? What did I already know? How did they conceal that information so that I knew it but didn't recognize its importance? And then how did they make that surprise pay off? What trick of cinematography or sound did they use? How did the person phrase the sentence that shocked me in the way it did? What move did the person make that convinced me, oh, I know what's about to happen now? The more you start to study surprise and the things that you watch, the more you pay attention to surprise. And I'm going to start you off with this ad. It's a Johnny Walker ad called Dear Brother. It's an outstanding example of surprise. I want you to watch it and first, register the emotional response that you have as a result of the surprise in the commercial. And then watch it again. Watch it more than once. Pay attention to the words, the music, and the actions. Pay attention to what the director did to cause you to be surprised at the end. What tricks were used to generate surprise? Pay attention carefully and then start doing it in your life. You read a book, you watch a movie, you watch a TV show, you're surprised, stop and say, how did they do that? Maybe you'll find strategies I have yet to find. If you do, let me know. But start paying attention to surprise in your life so that you can begin surprising people in your stories every day. Walking the roads of our youth through the land of our childhood, our home, and our truth. Be near me, guide me, always stay beside me so I can be free. Free. Let's roam this place familiar and vast, our playground of green frames, our past.

## [03:08 - 03:59]

We were wondrous, never lost, always humble. And every place was fanceless and time was endless. Our ways were always the same. Call my demons and walk with me, brother, until our roads lead us away from each other. And if your heart's full of sorrow, keep walking, don't rest, and promise me from heart to chest to never let your memories die. Never. I'll always be alive and by your side. In your mind, I'm free.
